title: MetricsReport
description: Measured results from a completed (or failed) experiment run. Links back to the ExperimentConfig via experiment_id (SHA-256 of the config). All latency/memory/quality fields are null when status is not 'completed'.

experiment_id.description: SHA-256 of the ExperimentConfig that produced this report (ExperimentConfig.content_hash()). Foreign key to the experiment ledger.

device_id.description: Foreign key to DeviceDescriptor.device_id. Identifies which device produced these measurements.

status.description: Outcome of the experiment run. 'oom' = out-of-memory before producing any output.

error_message.description: Error detail when status is not 'completed'. Null on success.

started_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone when the experiment run began.

completed_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone when the experiment run ended (success or failure).

hardware_fingerprint.description: Observed runtime state of the device at measurement time. Distinguishes from DeviceDescriptor (static capabilities) — this is the dynamic snapshot.

ttft_ms.description: Time to first token in milliseconds. Null when status is not 'completed'.

decode_tokens_per_sec.description: Steady-state decode throughput in tokens per second (measured after the first token). Null when status is not 'completed'.

peak_memory_mb.description: Peak RAM usage in megabytes during inference. Null when status is not 'completed'.

on_disk_size_mb.description: Total size of model files on disk in megabytes (weights + projector + any auxiliary files). Null when status is not 'completed'.

energy_j.description: Energy consumed during the benchmark run in joules. Null when not measured or when status is not 'completed'.

quality_scores.description: Per-benchmark quality measurements. Empty list when status is not 'completed'.

HardwareFingerprint.title: HardwareFingerprint
HardwareFingerprint.description: Observed runtime state of the device during measurement. Captures factors that can affect latency and memory readings: free RAM, thermal condition, OS version. Stored alongside every MetricsReport for reproducibility auditing.

os_version.description: OS version string at measurement time (e.g. 'iOS 18.3.1', 'Raspbian GNU/Linux 12', 'macOS 15.4').

available_ram_mb.description: Free RAM in megabytes immediately before the inference run. Values below ~500 MB on Pi 5 should be flagged — the measurement may be contaminated by swap.

thermal_state.description: Device thermal condition at measurement time. Free string; device-specific values (e.g. Apple: 'nominal'/'fair'/'serious'/'critical'; Pi: 'normal'/'throttled'). Null when not measurable.

swap_used_mb.description: Swap in use in MB at measurement time. None means swap was not measured. Non-zero means latency figures in the parent MetricsReport are unreliable. Downstream consumers (Pareto Tracker) should treat reports with non-zero swap as latency-suspect. Primarily relevant for Pi 5 4 GB.

BenchmarkScore.title: BenchmarkScore
BenchmarkScore.description: A single metric from a single benchmark evaluation. One MetricsReport may contain multiple BenchmarkScore entries (different benchmarks, different metrics within a benchmark).

benchmark.description: Benchmark name as reported by VLMEvalKit or the custom eval harness (e.g. 'RealWorldQA', 'MMBench_dev_en', 'POPE', 'stage_a_caption', 'stage_a_vqa').

value.description: Numeric metric value. Scale is benchmark-specific (accuracy is 0–1, CIDEr is typically 0–~5, etc.).
